extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won the sprint on rising finish after Quick-Step positioned him perfectly while VIVIANI struggled to find space in the finale.
tactical_patterns: Four teams (Lotto-Jumbo, Quick-Step, FDJ, Bahrain) shared chase work suggesting mutual interest in sprint rather than single team dominance.
Quick-Step's wheel discipline positioned Groenewegen on rising finish where explosiveness trumped pure speed, catching out Viviani's positioning.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: fifth win of season at age 24, demonstrating uphill sprint explosiveness on rising finishes beats pure speed specialists
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: struggled to find positioning in final 4km despite being fastest, suggests team leadout weakness or rider positioning errors
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: neutral: retained yellow jersey plus bonuses but had difficulty finding space in finale, vulnerable on non-pure-flat sprints
